growth_records.medium: Lake Rotsee / Lake Zug water (natural lake water)

up.compound: methane (CH4, as methane-carbon) - Lake Rotsee oxic
up.method: nanoSIMS single-cell (13C at% 28.77±4.1; assumed 2 um spherical, biovolume 4.2 um^3); cited from Oswald et al. 2015

up.compound: methane (CH4, as methane-carbon) - Lake Zug low O2
up.method: nanoSIMS single-cell (13C at% 10.39±3.1); cited from Oswald et al. 2016

up.compound: methane (CH4, as methane-carbon) - Lake Zug high O2
up.method: nanoSIMS single-cell (13C at% 12.13±3.75); cited from Oswald et al. 2016

growth_records.cite: Kirsten Oswald et al. Crenothrix are major methane consumers in stratified lakes. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.77. [PMC5563964]
growth_records.notes: Comparison unicellular gamma-MOB rates in Table 1 are cited FROM companion papers: Rotsee value (10.6, footnote h) from Oswald et al. 2015; Lake Zug low/high-O2 values (5.7, 6.9, footnote j) from Oswald et al. 2016. Per-cell rates 5-10x lower than filamentous Crenothrix. Population uptake Rotsee 0.27, Zug low 0.39, high 0.47 umol l^-1 d^-1. Units verbatim. Flagged low confidence because rates originate in cited works, not primary to this paper.
